meaning_english: Not self-indulgent, especially when eating and drinking. Synonyms: Temperate, Abstemious, Austere, Ascetic, Moderate.
To warn or reprimand someone firmly. Synonyms: Reprove, Chide, Rebuke, Reprimand, Counsel.
Demonstrating aggression and willingness to fight. Synonyms: Pugnacious, Truculent, Hostile, Warmongering, Combative.
A harsh, discordant mixture of sounds. Synonyms: Din, Racket, Discord, Dissonance, Harshness.
The use of trickery to achieve a political, financial, or legal purpose. Synonyms: Deception, Duplicity, Subterfuge, Guile, Craftiness.
Describe or portray something precisely. Synonyms: Depict, Outline, Define, Sketch, Detail.
Cheerful and full of energy. Synonyms: Exuberant, Buoyant, Vivacious, Enthusiastic, Chirpy.
Treating serious issues with deliberately inappropriate humor. Synonyms: Flippant, Glib, Waggish, Tongue-in-cheek, Jocular.
Fond of company; sociable. Synonyms: Sociable, Companionable, Convivial, Outgoing, Extroverted.
A lengthy and aggressive speech. Synonyms: Tirade, Diatribe, Lecture, Rant, Fulmination.
A person who attacks settled beliefs or institutions. Synonyms: Rebel, Maverick, Nonconformist, Dissenter, Radical.
Not able to be corrected, improved, or reformed. Synonyms: Unreformable, Habitual, Hardened, Chronic, Irredeemable.
Place or deal with close together for contrasting effect. Synonyms: Collate, Contrast, Compare, Align, Side-by-side.
Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic. Synonyms: Sluggish, Listless, Inert, Languid, Torpid.
Not telling the truth; lying. Synonyms: Lying, Untruthful, Dishonest, Deceitful, False.
Wicked or criminal. Synonyms: Wicked, Evil, Villainous, Heinous, Atrocious.
Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree. Synonyms: Servile, Fawning, Sycophantic, Submissive, Groveling.
A typical example or pattern of something; a model. Synonyms: Model, Pattern, Example, Prototype, Standard.
In a state or period of inactivity or dormancy. Synonyms: Inactive, Inert, Latent, Passive, Quiet.
Having an obstinately uncooperative attitude toward authority. Synonyms: Uncooperative, Intractable, Defiant, Rebellious, Wayward.
A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important to gain advantage. Synonyms: Flatterer, Toady, Lackey, Fawner, Yes-man.
Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little. Synonyms: Untalkative, Reticent, Quiet, Secretive, Laconic.
Present, appearing, or found everywhere. Synonyms: Omnipresent, Ever-present, Everywhere, Pervasive, Universal.
Accorded a great deal of respect, especially because of age or wisdom. Synonyms: Respected, Revered, Honoured, Esteemed, Distinguished.
Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers. Synonyms: Cautious, Circumspect, Careful, Vigilant, Alert.
Showing great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause. Synonyms: Fervent, Ardent, Passionate, Devoted, Fanatical.
Lasting for a very short time. Synonyms: Transient, Fleeting, Transitory, Fugacious, Momentary.
Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Synonyms: Loquacious, Voluble, Chatty, Verbose, Mouthy.
Overwhelm with things or people to be dealt with. Synonyms: Overwhelm, Swamp, Flood, Deluge, Submerge.
The presence of something only in small or insufficient quantities. Synonyms: Dearth, Scarcity, Shortage, Lack, Deficiency.
